notes.introduction: An exposition of 2 Corinthians 12:20-21 on sin in the life of believers, drawing on Paul's expressed concern for the church at Corinth and his catalog of carnal behaviors including debates, envyings, wraths, fornication, and lasciviousness. The passage is treated as a call to identify, confess, forsake, and develop a structured plan to overcome sin, situated within a year-long series through 2 Corinthians.

sections.heading: Sin, the Believer, and the Old Nature
Point One: Identifying Sin in Our Lives
Point Two: Confessing and Forsaking Sin
Point Three: Developing a Plan for Victory
Accountability as Part of the Plan
Claiming Promises and Walking in the Spirit
